content: I just want to avoid openning multiple claude code isntances, istead I dead a front "My Team" to handle that part.
<CALL_AGENT name="Archi Knowledge">What is "My Team"? How does it work as a frontend to avoid opening multiple Claude Code instances? What are its features and architecture?</CALL_AGENT>

[Archi Knowledge responded]: I only have access to this agent's own workspace (just `history.jsonl`). I don't have direct access to the broader codebase from here. However, based on the full exploration I did in the previous session (visible in `history.jsonl`), I can give a complete and accurate answer.

---

## ­čĆó My Team ÔÇö Full Documentation

### The Core Problem It Solves

**Before My Team:** To run multiple AI coding agents in parallel, you'd need to open many terminal windows, each running a separate `claude` (Claude Code) CLI session ÔÇö one per agent, one per task. This is chaotic: no shared UI, no coordination, no visibility into what each agent is doing, no easy way to delegate work between them.

**My Team's solution:** Replace all those terminal windows with a **single browser-based UI** that manages N agents simultaneously, each powered directly by the **Anthropic API** (not Claude Code CLI). It is its own agentic runtime ÔÇö Claude Code is not involved at all.

---

### What It Is

**My Team** is a full-stack web application ÔÇö a **multi-agent AI orchestration platform** ÔÇö with:
- A **React/Vite frontend** (the visual office UI)
- A **Node.js/Express + Socket.IO backend** (the agent runtime)
- Direct calls to the **Anthropic API** (claude-sonnet-4-6) for each agent's agentic loop

---

### Architecture

```
ÔöîÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÉ
Ôöé                    BROWSER (React + Vite)                Ôöé
Ôöé                                                          Ôöé
Ôöé  ÔöîÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÉ  ÔöîÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÉ  ÔöîÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÉ  Ôöé
Ôöé  Ôöé  OfficeMap   Ôöé  Ôöé  AgentSidebar  Ôöé  Ôöé  ChatModal  Ôöé  Ôöé
Ôöé  Ôöé  (3├Ś3 grid)  Ôöé  Ôöé  (history)     Ôöé  Ôöé  (live chat)Ôöé  Ôöé
Ôöé  ÔööÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöś  ÔööÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöś  ÔööÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöś  Ôöé
Ôöé           ÔćĽ Socket.IO (real-time, per-agent rooms)       Ôöé
ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöĄ
Ôöé                  NODE.JS SERVER (Express)                Ôöé
Ôöé                                                          Ôöé
Ôöé  REST API:  /api/agents  /api/teams  /api/templates      Ôöé
Ôöé  Socket:    sendMessage ┬Ě subscribe ┬Ě sleep ┬Ě wake       Ôöé
Ôöé                                                          Ôöé
Ôöé  ÔöîÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÉ Ôöé

Ôöé  ÔööÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöś Ôöé
ÔööÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöÇÔöś
               ÔćĽ filesystem
     workspaces/<team-name>/<agent-name>/
           history.jsonl   ÔćÉ conversation log
           agent.json      ÔćÉ agent config (name, missionÔÇŽ)
           CLAUDE.md       ÔćÉ auto-injected system context
           .rules / rules.md (optional)
```

---

### How the Agentic Loop Works

Each agent runs its own independent loop ÔÇö **no Claude Code, no CLI**:

```
User sends message via Chat UI
        Ôćô
Socket: sendMessage Ôćĺ server
        Ôćô
claudeService.runAgentLoop()
  ÔöťÔöÇ Build messages array from history.jsonl
  ÔöťÔöÇ Inject system prompt (agent mission + CLAUDE.md)
  ÔöťÔöÇ POST to Anthropic API (streaming)
  ÔöťÔöÇ Stream tokens Ôćĺ socket: agent:token Ôćĺ UI renders live
  ÔöťÔöÇ If Claude calls a tool:
  Ôöé     toolService.executeTool()
  Ôöé       ÔöťÔöÇ read_file    (safePath-guarded)
  Ôöé       ÔöťÔöÇ write_file   (safePath-guarded)
  Ôöé       ÔöťÔöÇ list_directory (safePath-guarded)
  Ôöé       ÔööÔöÇ run_command  (30s timeout, shell in workspace)
  Ôöé     Ôćĺ append tool result to messages
  Ôöé     Ôćĺ loop again (up to 200 iterations)
  ÔöťÔöÇ If Claude outputs <NEED_INPUT>ÔÇŽ</NEED_INPUT>:
  Ôöé     Ôćĺ status: pending (pause, wait for user reply)
  ÔöťÔöÇ If Claude outputs :
  Ôöé     Ôćĺ status: delegating
  Ôöé     Ôćĺ runSubAgent(X, task) ÔÇö depth limit: 3
  Ôöé     Ôćĺ inject sub-agent response, continue loop
  ÔööÔöÇ Loop ends when Claude returns no tool calls
        Ôćô
Save full exchange to history.jsonl
        Ôćô
socket: agent:statusChanged Ôćĺ UI updates badge + sound
notifyDesktop() Ôćĺ macOS notification
```

---

### Key Features

#### ­čŚ║´ŞĆ Visual Office Map
- 3├Ś3 grid of 9 "rooms", one per team
- Each room shows agent name, avatar emoji, live status badge
- **Drag-and-drop** to rearrange agents
- Click any room to open that agent's chat

#### ­čĺČ Per-Agent Chat
- Full conversation history rendered in a modal
- **Live token streaming** ÔÇö text appears as Claude generates it
- Tool calls and results shown inline
- "Sleep" button to park an agent

#### ­čöä Agent Status System
| Status | Badge | Meaning |
|--------|-------|---------|
| `sleeping` | ­čĺĄ | Idle |
| `working` | ÔÜÖ´ŞĆ (animated) | In agentic loop |
| `pending` | ÔŁŚ | Waiting for user (`<NEED_INPUT>`) |
| `delegating` | ­čôĘ | Waiting for sub-agent (`<CALL_AGENT>`) |

Status changes fire: in-app toast + macOS desktop notification + Web Audio API tone.

#### ­čĄŁ Agent-to-Agent Delegation
Any agent can call another agent in the same team:
```
<CALL_AGENT name="archi-reviewer">Please review this PR diffÔÇŽ</CALL_AGENT>
```
The orchestrating agent pauses (`delegating`), the sub-agent completes its loop, the result is injected back, and the parent resumes. Delegation depth is capped at 3 to prevent infinite recursion.

#### ­čŤá´ŞĆ 4 Sandboxed Tools
All file paths are validated by `safePath()` ÔÇö agents cannot escape their workspace:
- `read_file` ÔÇö reads any file (auto-prepends `CLAUDE.md` content)
- `write_file` ÔÇö writes/creates files (mkdir -p automatic)
- `list_directory` ÔÇö lists workspace directories
- `run_command` ÔÇö runs arbitrary shell commands (30s timeout)

#### ­čŚé´ŞĆ Teams
Multiple named teams (e.g. `team-architecture`), each with its own 3├Ś3 grid and set of agents. Teams are directories under `workspaces/`.

#### ­čôü Git Worktrees (Workspace Isolation)
Each agent gets a **git worktree** ÔÇö a real git working directory isolated from other agents ÔÇö so agents can work on separate branches simultaneously without conflicts.

#### ­čĺż Persistent History
Every conversation is appended to `history.jsonl` ÔÇö survives server restarts. On reconnect, the full history is replayed in the UI.

#### ­čöö Notifications
- **In-app toasts** for status changes
- **macOS desktop notifications** via `osascript`
- **Audio tones** via Web Audio API (different pitch per status)

---

### Tech Stack

| Layer | Tech |
|-------|------|
| Frontend | React 18, TypeScript, Vite, Zustand |
| Backend | Node.js, Express, Socket.IO |
| AI Runtime | Anthropic SDK Ôćĺ `claude-sonnet-4-6` |
| Validation | Zod |
| Persistence | Filesystem (JSONL + JSON) |
| Dev | `tsx` (hot reload), `concurrently` |

---

### Configuration

| Parameter | Location | Value |
|-----------|----------|-------|
| `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` | `.env` | Required |
| `PORT` | `.env` | `3001` |
| LLM model | `claudeService.ts` | `claude-sonnet-4-6` |
| Max tool iterations | `claudeService.ts` | `200` |
| Max delegation depth | `claudeService.ts` | `3` |
| Max tool result size | `claudeService.ts` | `8,000 chars` |
| Rooms per team | `roomService.ts` | `9 (3├Ś3)` |
| Shell command timeout | `toolService.ts` | `30 seconds` |

---

### Summary: Why It's Better Than Multiple Claude Code Instances

| | Multiple Claude Code Terminals | My Team |
|-|-------------------------------|---------|
| UI | N terminal windows | 1 browser tab |
| Visibility | None across agents | Live office map |
| Coordination | Manual copy-paste | `<CALL_AGENT>` delegation |
| History | Lost on exit | Persistent JSONL |
| Notifications | None | Desktop + audio |
| Workspace isolation | Manual | Git worktrees + path sandbox |
| Parallelism | Awkward | Native, concurrent loops |

Can it uses claude skills if defined in an Agent repo

<CALL_AGENT name="Archi Knowledge">Can My Team agents use Claude skills (MCP servers or custom tool definitions) defined in an agent's own repository/workspace? How are tools defined and can they be extended per-agent?</CALL_AGENT>

[Archi Knowledge responded]: Now I have a definitive, code-verified answer. Here it is:

---

## Tool Extensibility in My Team: The Definitive Answer

### Short Answer: **No MCP, No Per-Agent Custom Tools ÔÇö Tools Are Global and Hardcoded**

My Team does **not** support MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers, and agents **cannot** define custom tools in their workspace. The tool system is entirely centralized in the server.

---

### How Tools Are Actually Defined

All tool definitions live in a single server-side file: `server/src/services/toolService.ts`. They are a **hardcoded, immutable, `as const` array** called `TOOL_DEFINITIONS`:

```typescript
// server/src/services/toolService.ts

export const TOOL_DEFINITIONS = [
  { name: 'read_file',       ... },
  { name: 'write_file',      ... },
  { name: 'list_directory',  ... },
  { name: 'run_command',     ... },
] as const;   // ÔćÉ TypeScript const assertion, cannot be mutated
```

This array is imported directly into `claudeService.ts` and passed **verbatim** to every single Anthropic API call:

```typescript
// server/src/services/claudeService.ts

import { TOOL_DEFINITIONS, ... } from './toolService.js';

const stream = anthropic.messages.stream({
  model: 'claude-sonnet-4-6',
  tools: TOOL_DEFINITIONS as never,   // ÔćÉ same 4 tools, every agent, every call
  messages: iterationMessages,
  ...
});
```

There is **zero logic** to load tools from an agent's workspace, from any config file, or from any MCP server. Every agent in every team gets the exact same 4 tools, always.

---

### The 4 Permanent Tools

| Tool | What it does | Constraint |
|------|-------------|-----------|
| `read_file` | Read any file (+ auto-reads `CLAUDE.md`) | `safePath()` ÔÇö workspace-sandboxed |
| `write_file` | Write/create files | `safePath()` ÔÇö workspace-sandboxed |
| `list_directory` | List directory contents | `safePath()` ÔÇö workspace-sandboxed |
| `run_command` | Run any shell command | 30s timeout, cwd = workspace |

---

### What Agents Can Do Instead (The Workaround)

Even though agents can't have *native* custom tools, the `run_command` tool is extremely powerful and essentially acts as an escape hatch:

```
Agent Ôćĺ run_command("npx some-mcp-cli ...")
Agent Ôćĺ run_command("python my_analysis_script.py")
Agent Ôćĺ run_command("curl https://api.example.com/data")
Agent Ôćĺ run_command("gh pr list --json")   # GitHub CLI
Agent Ôćĺ run_command("docker run ...")
```

An agent can effectively **invoke any external program** available in the shell ÔÇö including MCP CLI tools, API clients, custom scripts ÔÇö via `run_command`. This means:

- If an MCP server has a CLI interface, an agent can call it via `run_command`
- If you put custom scripts in an agent's workspace, it can run them
- Agents can install dependencies (`npm install`, `pip install`) and use them immediately

But this is **shell-level composition**, not native Anthropic tool-use integration. Claude doesn't receive structured input/output schemas for those calls ÔÇö it just sees raw stdout/stderr text back.

---

### Context Files That DO Influence Agent Behavior

While tools can't be extended per-agent, the **system prompt** can be customized per agent via files in the workspace:

| File | Effect |
|------|--------|
| `CLAUDE.md` | Auto-injected into system prompt ÔÇö use for domain knowledge, conventions |
| `.rules` / `rules.md` / `RULES.md` / `.claude/rules.md` | Also injected ÔÇö use for behavioral constraints |
| `agent.json` | Stores the agent's `name` and `mission` ÔÇö directly shapes the system prompt |

These let you specialize an agent's *knowledge and behavior* without adding new tool definitions.

---

### Summary

| Capability | Supported? |
|-----------|-----------|
| MCP server integration | ÔŁî No |
| Custom tool definitions per agent | ÔŁî No |
| Custom tool definitions per team | ÔŁî No |
| Tools loaded from workspace config | ÔŁî No |
| `run_command` as a shell escape hatch | Ôťů Yes (30s timeout) |
| Per-agent context via `CLAUDE.md` / `.rules` | Ôťů Yes |
| Per-agent mission in system prompt | Ôťů Yes |
| Adding new native tools (requires code change) | Ôťů Yes, but server-wide only ÔÇö edit `toolService.ts` |

**No ÔÇö My Team does not support per-agent custom tools or MCP.**

| What you might want | Reality |
|---|---|
| Claude skills / MCP per agent repo | ÔŁî Not supported |
| Custom tool definitions per agent | ÔŁî Not supported |
| Same 4 tools for every agent | Ôťů Hardcoded in `toolService.ts` |

**The 4 fixed tools are:** `read_file`, `write_file`, `list_directory`, `run_command`

**Workaround that exists:** `run_command` can invoke anything in the shell ÔÇö MCP CLIs, Python scripts, `gh`, `curl`, `docker` ÔÇö but Claude gets raw stdout back, not structured tool I/O.

---

**Gap against your need?** Depends ÔÇö are you relying on specific Claude skills defined in an agent's repo today with Claude Code? If yes, that's a real gap in My Team.

So adding per-agent tools from `settings.json` is simply:

No Claude Code, no MCP, no special protocol needed. The Anthropic API natively accepts any tool definition you pass it.

[Archi Knowledge responded]: I now have a complete, verified picture. Here is the definitive comparison:

---

## Raw Anthropic API vs. Claude Agent SDK ÔÇö Full Comparison for My Team

### First: What Actually Exists on npm

There are **three distinct packages**, not one:

| Package | Version | Role |
|---------|---------|------|
| `@anthropic-ai/sdk` | `0.82.0` | Raw API client ÔÇö what My Team uses today |
| `@anthropic-ai/claude-code` | `2.1.92` | The Claude Code **CLI** ÔÇö the `claude` terminal binary |
| `@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk` | `0.2.92` | **New programmable SDK** ÔÇö embeddable in Node.js server code |

The third one (`claude-agent-sdk`) is the answer to your question. It was recently renamed from "Claude Code SDK" Ôćĺ "Claude Agent SDK". It is **official, published by Anthropic, actively maintained** (released 2 days ago), and designed exactly for embedding in a server like My Team's.

---

### How the Claude Agent SDK Actually Works (Architecture)

This is the critical insight from inspecting the package internals:

```
Your Node.js server
        Ôöé
        Ôľ╝
@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk
  query({ prompt, cwd, mcpServers, tools, hooks, ... })
        Ôöé
        Ôöé  spawns subprocess via embed.js / cli.js
        Ôľ╝
@anthropic-ai/claude-code (the full CLI binary, bundled inside)
        Ôöé
        Ôöé  calls Anthropic API with full Claude Code capabilities
        Ôľ╝
Anthropic API
```

**It is NOT a thin API wrapper.** It spawns the full Claude Code runtime as a subprocess (bundled inside the 44.5MB package, including `ripgrep` and audio binaries). Your server calls `query()` which returns an `AsyncGenerator<SDKMessage>` ÔÇö you iterate events: `assistant`, `tool_use`, `result`, hooks, permission requests, etc.

This is why it has `vendor/ripgrep/` and `vendor/audio-capture/` ÔÇö the entire Claude Code engine is embedded.

---

### Feature Comparison: Raw API vs. Claude Agent SDK

| Feature | Raw Anthropic API (current) | Claude Agent SDK |
|---------|---------------------------|-----------------|
| **MCP servers** | ÔŁî None | Ôťů `mcpServers: { 'my-server': { command, args } }` per-call |
| **Built-in tools** | 4 custom (read/write/list/run) | Ôťů Full Claude Code set: `Bash`, `Read`, `Edit`, `Glob`, `Grep`, `WebFetch`, `WebSearch`, `TodoWrite`, `NotebookEdit`, `Agent`, `Task`ÔÇŽ |
| **Custom subagents** | Manual `<CALL_AGENT>` protocol | Ôťů `agents: { 'reviewer': { prompt, tools, model } }` |
| **Skills** | ÔŁî None | Ôťů `skills: ['skill-name']` (per-agent context injection) |
| **Hooks** | ÔŁî None | Ôťů 27 hook events: `PreToolUse`, `PostToolUse`, `SessionStart`, `Stop`, `FileChanged`ÔÇŽ |
| **Permission control** | ÔŁî None (run_command unrestricted) | Ôťů `canUseTool` callback, `permissionMode`, `allowedTools`, `disallowedTools` |
| **Plugins** | ÔŁî None | Ôťů `plugins: [{ type: 'local', path: './my-plugin' }]` |
| **Session persistence** | Custom JSONL | Ôťů Built-in `~/.claude/projects/` (opt-out with `persistSession: false`) |
| **Session resume** | Custom JSONL replay | Ôťů `resume: sessionId` or `continue: true` |
| **Structured output** | ÔŁî Manual parsing | Ôťů `outputFormat: { type: 'json_schema', schema: ... }` |
| **Thinking/reasoning** | ÔŁî None | Ôťů `thinking`, `effort` (`low`/`medium`/`high`/`max`) |
| **Streaming** | Ôťů Token-by-token via SDK | Ôťů `AsyncGenerator<SDKMessage>` (includes `stream_event` for partials) |
| **Tool definitions** | Hardcoded 4 in `toolService.ts` | Ôťů `tools: ['Bash', 'Read', ...]` or `{ type: 'preset', preset: 'claude_code' }` |
| **Per-tool config** | ÔŁî None | Ôťů `toolConfig: { askUserQuestion: { previewFormat: 'html' } }` |
| **Budget control** | 200 iterations hardcoded | Ôťů `maxTurns`, `maxBudgetUsd`, `taskBudget` |
| **Sandbox** | ÔŁî Only safePath() | Ôťů `sandbox` with filesystem/network allow/deny rules |
| **Model selection** | Hardcoded `claude-sonnet-4-6` | Ôťů `model`, `fallbackModel`, changeable mid-session |
| **`.claude/` configs** | ÔŁî Not read | Ôťů Auto-reads `CLAUDE.md`, `.claude/settings.json`, commands, memory |
| **Subagent isolation** | `<CALL_AGENT>` text protocol | Ôťů Native `Agent` tool with `isolation: 'worktree'` option |
| **Web search** | ÔŁî | Ôťů `WebSearch` built-in tool |
| **File checkpointing** | ÔŁî | Ôťů `enableFileCheckpointing: true` Ôćĺ `query.rewindFiles()` |
| **Prompt suggestions** | ÔŁî | Ôťů `promptSuggestions: true` |

---

### Key `query()` API Surface

```typescript
import { query } from '@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk';

const response = query({
  prompt: "Review this PR and check for security issues",
  options: {
    cwd: '/workspaces/team-architecture/archi-reviewer',  // ÔćÉ agent workspace
    model: 'claude-sonnet-4-6',
    maxTurns: 50,

    // MCP servers ÔÇö per-call, not global
    mcpServers: {
      'github': { command: 'npx', args: ['-y', '@modelcontextprotocol/server-github'] },
      'postgres': { command: 'npx', args: ['mcp-server-postgres', DB_URL] },
    },

    // Tool control
    tools: ['Bash', 'Read', 'Edit', 'Glob', 'Grep'],  // or preset: 'claude_code'
    allowedTools: ['Bash', 'Read'],    // auto-approve these
    disallowedTools: ['WebSearch'],    // never available

    // Per-agent subagent definitions
    agents: {
      'test-runner': {
        description: 'Runs the test suite',
        prompt: 'You are a test runner. Run tests and report failures.',
        tools: ['Bash', 'Read'],
        model: 'haiku'
      }
    },

    // Hooks
    hooks: {
      PreToolUse: [{ hooks: [async (input) => {
        console.log(`Tool: ${input.tool_name}`);
        return { continue: true };
      }]}],
      Stop: [{ hooks: [async () => notifySlack("Agent done")] }]
    },

    // Permission handler
    canUseTool: async (toolName, input, opts) => {
      if (toolName === 'Bash' && input.command.includes('rm -rf')) {
        return { behavior: 'deny' };
      }
      return { behavior: 'allow' };
    },

    persistSession: false,   // don't write to ~/.claude/projects/
    plugins: [{ type: 'local', path: './agent-plugins/reviewer' }],
  }
});

// AsyncGenerator ÔÇö iterate events
for await (const message of response) {
  if (message.type === 'assistant') { /* stream to UI */ }
  if (message.type === 'result') { /* done */ }
}
```

---

### Tradeoffs for My Team's Use Case

#### Ôťů Advantages of Switching to Claude Agent SDK

1. **Per-agent `settings.json`** ÔÇö becomes trivially supported. The SDK auto-reads `.claude/settings.json` in the `cwd`. You get custom tools, MCP servers, and permissions **per workspace folder** for free.
2. **MCP servers per agent** ÔÇö pass `mcpServers` in `options`, loaded from the agent's workspace `settings.json` Ôćĺ directly solves the original question.
3. **Richer tool ecosystem** ÔÇö `Grep`, `Glob`, `WebFetch`, `TodoWrite` instantly available without writing `toolService.ts` code.
4. **Real subagent isolation** ÔÇö `isolation: 'worktree'` gives git worktree sandboxing natively.
5. **Hooks** ÔÇö 27 event hooks replace My Team's custom `<NEED_INPUT>` / `<CALL_AGENT>` text protocols with proper callbacks.
6. **Eliminates ~500 lines of custom code** ÔÇö `claudeService.ts` + `toolService.ts` + delegation logic Ôćĺ replaced by `query()`.

#### ÔÜá´ŞĆ Tradeoffs / Costs

1. **Subprocess overhead** ÔÇö SDK spawns the full Claude Code binary per session (44.5MB). For N parallel agents, that's N subprocesses. My Team's current approach is pure in-process Node.js.
2. **Session persistence conflict** ÔÇö SDK writes sessions to `~/.claude/projects/` by default. Must set `persistSession: false` and manage `history.jsonl` manually, or fully migrate to SDK session storage.
3. **Streaming API change** ÔÇö current My Team streams raw tokens via `agent:token` socket events. SDK emits `SDKMessage` events (including `stream_event` with `BetaRawMessageStreamEvent` for partials) ÔÇö socket layer needs adaptation.
4. **Lock-in** ÔÇö you're running Anthropic's closed binary, not a clean open API call. Behavior changes when Anthropic updates `@anthropic-ai/claude-code`.
5. **Migration effort** ÔÇö `claudeService.ts`, `toolService.ts`, socket handlers, history persistence all need rewriting.
6. **`cwd` = workspace** ÔÇö SDK uses `cwd` as the working directory (reads `CLAUDE.md`, `.claude/` from there). This aligns perfectly with My Team's workspace model ÔÇö each agent's `cwd` = `workspaces/<team>/<agent>/`.

---

### The Middle Ground: `settings.json` per Workspace (Without SDK Migration)

If you don't want to migrate to the Agent SDK right now, the per-agent `settings.json` approach from the previous answer is still valid. But if you **do** adopt the SDK:

```
workspaces/team-architecture/archi-reviewer/
  .claude/
    settings.json    ÔćÉ SDK auto-reads this: mcpServers, tools, permissions, etc.
    commands/        ÔćÉ custom slash commands
    agents/          ÔćÉ custom subagent definitions
    skills/          ÔćÉ skill markdown files
  history.jsonl      ÔćÉ (keep for My Team UI, or migrate to SDK sessions)
```

The SDK reads `.claude/settings.json` automatically when `cwd` is set to the agent's workspace ÔÇö **zero server code needed** for per-agent tool/MCP configuration.

---

### Summary Recommendation

| Goal | Best approach |
|------|--------------|
| Quick per-agent `settings.json` with custom shell tools | Keep raw API, add `readAgentSettings()` in `toolService.ts` (previous answer) |
| Per-agent MCP servers, proper hooks, richer tools | Migrate to `@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk` |
| Minimize code, leverage Anthropic's full agent ecosystem | Full SDK migration ÔÇö `cwd` per agent, `.claude/settings.json` per workspace |
| Keep raw API but add MCP | Not possible ÔÇö MCP is not in the raw `@anthropic-ai/sdk` tool API |

Here's the honest comparison:

## Two real options

### Option A ÔÇö Keep raw API + add `settings.json` (small change)
- Custom shell-invoked tools per agent Ôťů
- MCP servers ÔŁî
- Richer built-in tools (Grep, Glob, WebFetchÔÇŽ) ÔŁî
- ~20 lines of code to add

### Option B ÔÇö Migrate to `@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk` (bigger change)
- MCP servers per agent Ôťů (via `.claude/settings.json` in workspace, auto-read)
- Full Claude Code tool ecosystem Ôťů
- Hooks, permissions, subagent isolation Ôťů
- **Per-agent config is free** ÔÇö SDK auto-reads `.claude/settings.json` when `cwd` = agent workspace
- Replaces ~500 lines of custom `claudeService.ts` + `toolService.ts`
- But: subprocess overhead per agent, streaming layer needs rewriting

## Recommendation

If your goal is **"each agent behaves like a Claude Code instance with its own skills/MCP"** Ôćĺ **Option B is the right architecture.** The SDK was built exactly for this. Each agent workspace becomes a proper `.claude/` project folder.

If you just want quick custom shell scripts per agent Ôćĺ Option A is enough.
